# UX Checklist: Troubleshooting, Security, Trust, Update, And Rollback

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-008 requirements quality for scannable support flows, route distinction, row-level next actions, and correct handoffs among install, reference, troubleshooting, security/trust, and update/rollback content.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are troubleshooting matrix fields complete enough for support use without requiring users to infer symptom, likely cause, inspect command or file, fix, platform, or follow-up destination? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Spec SC-002]
- [x] CHK002 Are all required troubleshooting categories specified, including install failure, marketplace source, generated payload, installed cache/runtime state, permissions/approvals, Spec Kit CLI, GitHub CLI, jq, Codex custom agents, path confusion, version drift, and source-vs-generated-payload mismatch? [Completeness, Spec FR-004]
- [x] CHK003 Does the spec define route-level responsibilities clearly enough to prevent troubleshooting, security/trust, and update/rollback pages from duplicating each other or sending users to the wrong page? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-002, Spec FR-007, Spec FR-011]
- [x] CHK004 Does the plan declare a concrete hand-authored reference-page handoff, or an explicit generator change, so reference readers can reach the correct DOC-008 troubleshooting, trust, or update/rollback destination without hand-editing generated reference subpages? [Spec FR-017, Plan Declared File Operations]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is the inspect-command/file requirement specific enough that a reader can identify the next read-only command, detail view, path, or source/reference link without reading the whole page? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Spec Key Entities]
- [x] CHK006 Are mutating recovery steps clearly separated from read-only inspection so the matrix does not make install, reload, remove, approve, edit, delete, rebuild, or cache mutation look like diagnosis? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-012, Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK007 Are platform labels constrained to `Claude Code`, `Codex`, or `Both`, with platform-specific text only where commands, files, reloads, restarts, or policies differ? [Clarity, Spec FR-005]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 Do update/rollback requirements align with troubleshooting requirements by preserving the same source, generated payload, installed runtime/cache, managed policy, and custom-agent distinctions? [Consistency, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-012, Spec FR-015, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK009 Do install-page link requirements and sidebar/navigation expectations align with the plan's docs-only file operations and existing Starlight route model? [Consistency, Spec FR-017, Plan Declared File Operations]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK010 Are scanability outcomes measurable through row completeness, required category coverage, internal link resolution, and validation commands rather than subjective wording like "easy" or "clear"? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-001, Spec SC-002, Spec SC-008]
- [x] CHK011 Are route-linking outcomes measurable enough to verify that install pages, reference handoffs, and the three DOC-008 pages send readers to the correct next destination? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec FR-017, Spec SC-001]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK012 Are empty or unresolved cases routed to the correct owner page or future spec instead of being left as generic dead ends? [Coverage, Spec FR-012, Spec PR Review Packet Requirements]
- [x] CHK013 Are evaluator flows covered separately from user support flows, including when a reader should leave troubleshooting and read security/trust evidence instead? [Coverage, Spec US2, Spec FR-007, Spec FR-010]
- [x] CHK014 Are returning-user stale install flows covered separately from first-install flows, including refresh, reinstall, remove, rollback, stale payload, stale cache, and version-sync cases? [Coverage, Spec US3, Spec FR-011]
